title: nemexia lasioneuron
side_effects: Nemexia lasioneuron can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased heart rate, hallucinations, and severe gastrointestinal issues. Ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, often accompanied by intense abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's vapors can cause respiratory problems, including wheezing and shortness of breath. Additionally, improper use of the nemexia lasioneuron plant may result in liver and kidney damage, which can be irreversible in severe cases.
